lesson.menu_desc: Chairing, managing the floor and verb patterns -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Leading a Meeting -- Chairing & Verb Patterns
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 17 -- Leading a Meeting

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel from the legal team, with a quick briefing before the meeting you will chair on Monday. The objective is to approve the project plan and the budget. We have apologies from two attendees, so they cannot come. I suggest allocating ten minutes to each item on the agenda, and I recommend avoiding going over time on the first topic, because it always takes longer than people expect. Invite each attendee to speak in turn, and when the team is ready, propose to vote on the plan. At the end, please summarize the decisions, agree on the next steps with the partner, and then adjourn the meeting. You will facilitate it very well. Good luck, and let me know how it goes.

extra_audio.text: First, the chair opens the meeting and states the objective and the apologies. Next, the chair suggests allocating time to each item. Then, during the discussion, the chair invites each attendee to speak, avoids going over time, and proposes to vote when the team is ready to proceed. After that, the team agrees to proceed with the plan. Finally, the chair summarizes the decisions, confirms the next steps, and adjourns the meeting.
